evidence: toolsets.py: get_toolset() is a single return TOOLSETS.get(name) with 10-line Args/Returns docstring; get_all_toolsets(), get_toolset_names(), validate_toolset() follow the same pattern of 6-10 line docstrings on 1-2 line trivial bodies

toolsets.py: restating comments that echo the code: '# Return toolset definition' above return TOOLSETS.get(name), '# Get toolset definition' above TOOLSETS.get(name), '# Collect direct tools' above tools = set(toolset.get('tools', []))
toolset_distributions.py: same docstring bloat pattern as toolsets.py -- get_distribution(), list_distributions(), validate_distribution() are all 1-line bodies with verbose multi-section docstrings

toolset_distributions.py: inline probability comments contradict their values -- 'image_gen: 90, # 80% chance', 'web: 97, # 15% chance of web search/scrape for documentation' -- stale comments from copied/edited AI output

Identical .env loading boilerplate (try/except UnicodeDecodeError with utf-8/latin-1 fallback) copied verbatim across 7+ entry-point files: cli.py, run_agent.py, gateway/run.py, hermes_cli/main.py, hermes_cli/doctor.py, rl_cli.py, cron/scheduler.py

Positive: module-level docstrings across the codebase are genuinely informative -- auxiliary_client.py explains resolution chains, tools/registry.py documents import ordering for circular-import safety, hermes_time.py documents fallback behavior
Positive: no noisy entry/exit debug logging pattern -- logger calls are purposeful and targeted at errors and key lifecycle events
Positive: section dividers (# ===, # ---, # ──) used consistently as navigation aids in large files, not as ceremony

ai_generated_debt.dimension_character: The codebase is largely free of AI-generated noise in its core modules -- module docstrings are informative, comments explain non-obvious constraints, and logging is purposeful rather than ceremonial. However, two modules (toolsets.py and toolset_distributions.py) exhibit textbook LLM-hallmark patterns: verbose Args/Returns docstrings on trivial one-line functions, restating comments, and stale probability comments that contradict their actual values. The duplicated .env loading boilerplate across 7+ files is a cross-cutting instance of copied-verbatim code that a human would have extracted into a shared helper.
ai_generated_debt.score_rationale: The core codebase (gateway, agent/, tools/registry, hermes_state, hermes_time) is clean and purpose-driven, which would support a score in the high 80s. However, toolsets.py and toolset_distributions.py have concentrated docstring bloat and restating comments that a developer must wade through, and the stale mismatched comments in toolset_distributions.py are a reliability concern. The 7-file .env loading duplication adds a boilerplate tax that pulls the score into the high 70s -- a developer working here would encounter these patterns regularly in certain areas but find most of the codebase respectably clean.

issues.summary: toolsets.py has verbose multi-section docstrings on trivial 1-2 line functions
evidence: get_toolset() is 'return TOOLSETS.get(name)' with 10-line Args/Returns docstring
get_all_toolsets() is 'return TOOLSETS.copy()' with 6-line docstring
get_toolset_names() is 'return list(TOOLSETS.keys())' with 6-line docstring
validate_toolset() has 8-line docstring for 2-line body
validate_distribution() in toolset_distributions.py mirrors the same pattern
issues.suggestion: Replace verbose Args/Returns docstrings with single-line docstrings. For example, get_toolset() needs only: """Return toolset definition by name, or None.""" -- the types are already in the signature annotations.

issues.summary: toolsets.py contains inline comments that restate the code without adding insight
evidence: Line 311: '# Return toolset definition' above 'return TOOLSETS.get(name)'
Line 349: '# Get toolset definition' above 'toolset = TOOLSETS.get(name)'
Line 354: '# Collect direct tools' above 'tools = set(toolset.get("tools", []))'
Line 342: '# Check for cycles' above 'if name in visited:'
issues.suggestion: Delete these restating comments. The code is self-explanatory -- 'return TOOLSETS.get(name)' does not need a '# Return toolset definition' caption.

issues.summary: toolset_distributions.py has inline probability comments that contradict their actual values
evidence: Line 48: 'image_gen: 90,  # 80% chance of image generation tools' (90 != 80)
Line 49: 'vision: 90,  # 60% chance of vision tools' (90 != 60)
Line 50: 'web: 55,  # 40% chance of web tools' (55 != 40)
Line 202: 'browser: 75,  # 10% chance' (75 != 10)
issues.suggestion: Remove all these inline probability comments -- the numeric values are self-documenting and the stale comments are actively misleading. If comments are kept, they should describe the reasoning for the weight, not restate the number.

issues.summary: Identical .env loading try/except boilerplate duplicated across 7+ entry-point files
evidence: The pattern 'try: load_dotenv(..., encoding="utf-8") except UnicodeDecodeError: load_dotenv(..., encoding="latin-1")' appears identically in all 7 files
Several files also share the _hermes_home / _user_env / _project_env variable setup before the dotenv call
Each file also duplicates 'os.environ.setdefault("MSWEA_GLOBAL_CONFIG_DIR", str(_hermes_home))'
issues.suggestion: Extract a shared load_hermes_env() helper (e.g., in hermes_constants.py or a new hermes_bootstrap.py) that encapsulates the env file resolution, encoding fallback, and MSWEA_GLOBAL_CONFIG_DIR setup. Each entry point then calls one function instead of duplicating 10+ lines.

root_causes: toolsets.py and toolset_distributions.py appear to have been generated with verbose LLM defaults and not edited down -- the docstring style there is markedly different from the rest of the codebase
The .env loading boilerplate was likely copy-pasted as each new entry point was created, with no refactoring pass to extract a shared helper
likely_symptoms: stale_probability_comments is a symptom of the docstring bloat pattern -- when values change, the restating comments go stale

add.description: Module docstrings in agent/, gateway/, tools/registry.py, and utility modules explain architecture, resolution chains, import ordering rationale, and non-obvious constraints -- genuinely useful context that aids navigation.

add.header: Purposeful logging with no noisy entry/exit pattern
add.description: logger.getLogger(__name__) is used pervasively but logging calls are targeted at errors and key lifecycle events. No entry/exit debug logging or full object dumps observed.

add.header: Section dividers as navigation aids in large files
add.description: Consistent use of # === / # --- / # -- dividers in files like run_agent.py, hermes_state.py, tools/registry.py provides visual structure without ceremony.

add.header: Docstring bloat concentrated in toolsets modules
add.description: toolsets.py and toolset_distributions.py have verbose multi-section docstrings on trivial 1-2 line functions, contrasting with the rest of the codebase where docstrings are proportionate to function complexity.

add.header: Duplicated bootstrap boilerplate across entry points
add.description: The .env loading pattern (try utf-8, except latin-1 fallback) plus MSWEA env setup is copied verbatim across 7+ files rather than extracted to a shared helper.
